channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising State-Sponsored Hostage-Taking: Emerging Research and Policy Responses
item.description: Hostage-taking by nation-states is becoming a growing international concern, moving beyond its traditional association with non-state actors like terrorist groups and criminal organisations. Countries...

item.title: Transport group accuses DOTr officials of irregular Stradcom contract extension
item.description: A transport advocacy group, Coalition 169, has lodged formal complaints against both former and current officials of the Department of Transportation (DOTr) over the alleged improper extension of Stra...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Missile and Drone Attacks on UAE, Urges Regional Stability
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has expressed Pakistan’s strong condemnation of recent missile and drone attacks on the United Arab Emirates, showing full support for the Emirati leadership and citizens...

item.title: Health Department Assures Public as Hantavirus Cases Reported on Cruise Ship
item.description: The South African Health Department has reassured the public following reports of hantavirus infections on the Dutch-registered MV Hondius, which was en route from southern Argentina to the Canary Isl...

item.title: Moniepoint CEO cites skill gaps as reason for unfilled vacancies, sparking public debate
item.description: Moniepoint CEO, Tosin Eniolorunda, don yarn say dem get over 500 job vacancies wey dem never fit fill because local Nigerians no get the skills wey the company dey find. He talk say the wahala dey due...

item.title: Senator Cherargei Proposes Review of Former President Uhuru Kenyatta’s Retirement Benefits
item.description: Nandi Senator Samson Cherargei has tabled a motion in Parliament seeking to remove or review the retirement benefits of former President Uhuru Kenyatta. The motion comes amid concerns over the country...

item.title: Tennis Icons Blend Fashion and Advocacy at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Serena Williams, Venus Williams and Naomi Osaka show strong presence for the 2026 Met Gala, where sport meet fashion and social message. The event happen for the Metropolitan Museum of Art under the t...

item.title: Civil rights groups challenge Texas law expanding state immigration enforcement
item.description: A coalition of civil rights organizations, including the Texas Civil Rights Project, the ACLU, and the ACLU of Texas, has filed a new lawsuit seeking to block major parts of Texas Senate Bill 4. The l...

item.title: Yanga, Simba, and Azam in Tight Race After Kariakoo Derby Draw
item.description: Dar es Salaam's Premier League season is heating up after the Kariakoo derby ended 2-2 between Young Africans (Yanga) and Simba SC. After 20 matches, Yanga still lead with 48 points, keeping their unb...

item.title: Supreme Court urges amendment of MTP law to address challenges of unwanted pregnancies among minors
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has observed that cases of unwanted pregnancies, especially among minors, are increasing and stressed the need to amend the Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP) law to add...

item.title: Experts Warn of Declining Global Oil Reserves and Potential Energy Shortages
item.description: Experts are raising concerns over what they describe as a severe depletion of global oil reserves, warning that the world could be facing unprecedented energy challenges. The decline in oil production...

item.title: Survey shows many parents worry about daughters' safety in public spaces
item.description: A recent survey has revealed that a significant proportion of parents are concerned about the safety of their daughters when outside in public. The study highlights widespread fears regarding potentia...

item.title: Analysis of UK airports with the highest flight delays
item.description: The Sky News article reports on the airports in the United Kingdom experiencing the most significant delays for passengers. While the exact data from the source is unavailable due to access restrictio...

item.title: Proteas Women target middle-order improvement and spin handling before T20 World Cup
item.description: The Proteas Women’s team are sharpening their strategy ahead of the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, with a clear focus on improving their performance during the middle overs and handling spin bowling more ...

item.title: Many UK pubs facing closure amid rising taxes
item.description: The UK pub industry is facing a significant crisis as a growing number of establishments are forced to shut their doors. Reports indicate that two pubs are closing every day, largely due to the rising...

item.title: Trump Family’s Crypto Venture Files Lawsuit Against Investor Over Token Dispute
item.description: Washington: The crypto venture World Liberty Financial, co-founded by US President Donald Trump and his sons, has filed a defamation lawsuit against Hong Kong-based crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun. The...

item.title: FCT High Court to Rule on SSS N5 Billion Defamation Case Against SERAP
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court, Abuja, dey set to deliver judgement on Tuesday, 5 May 2026, for the N5 billion defamation case wey the State Security Service (SSS) file against the civ...

item.title: Kano State Credits FG Partnership for Progress in Roads, Education, and Health
item.description: Kano State government don yarn say di improvements wey dem don see for infrastructure, education, and healthcare na because of correct collaboration with di federal government. During one media tour o...

item.title: Kenya High Commission Advises Citizens in South Africa to Stay Safe During Protests
item.description: The Kenya High Commission in Pretoria has issued a safety advisory for Kenyans living in South Africa due to recent protests that have targeted foreign nationals in some areas. Citizens are being urge...

item.title: COA Confirms Allowance Payment for Illegally Dismissed Zamboanga Sur Official
item.description: The Commission on Audit (COA) has ruled in favor of former Zamboanga del Sur provincial accountant Ma. Eden Tagayuna, confirming that she is entitled to nearly P2.5 million in representation and trans...
